one_line: Maratha rebel general and master tactician of the 1857 uprising in Bundelkhand.

appearance.age_look: Mid-30s, lean and wiry with a trained soldier’s build and light battlefield scars.
appearance.face: Oval face with sharp cheekbones, wheatish-brown complexion, strong jaw, long straight nose, thick arched brows, intense almond-shaped eyes, and a prominent, well-groomed Maratha-style moustache; default expression is calm, focused resolve with a hint of stern determination.
appearance.hair: Black hair grown to medium length but neatly tied back and hidden under a traditional off-white Maratha-style turban with a slight forward tilt and a narrow red band wrapped once around its base.
appearance.costume: Era-accurate Maratha officer attire: a knee-length off-white cotton angarkha (front-wrapped robe) with a subtle vertical weave, tied at the right side with thin cords; beneath, a plain, slightly off-white dhoti wrapped in the Maharashtrian style and pleated in front, ending mid-calf; a deep maroon cotton sash (kamarband) snugly wrapped around the waist with one short end hanging slightly over the left hip; fitted long sleeves with simple cuffs; a light beige cotton shawl folded and draped over the left shoulder and around the back for travel; bare feet in simple brown leather kolhapuri-style sandals; minimal jewellery limited to a thin dark metal bangle on the right wrist and a small, unobtrusive black thread around the neck.
appearance.signature: A slightly curved Indo-Persian talwar with a steel blade, brass hilt, disc pommel, and simple brown leather scabbard slung from his maroon waist sash, often held in his right hand with the scabbard hanging at his left hip.

ref_desc: Tatya Tope is a lean mid-30s Maratha general with wheatish skin, sharp features, a prominent moustache, black hair under an off-white turban, wearing an off-white angarkha and dhoti with a maroon sash and beige shawl, carrying a brass-hilted talwar in a brown scabbard.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style character sheet of Tatya Tope as a dignified Maratha rebel general: a lean mid-30s man with wheatish-brown skin, sharp cheekbones, strong jaw, long straight nose, thick brows, intense almond-shaped eyes, and a prominent well-groomed moustache, his default expression calm and resolute. His black hair is medium length but tied back and hidden beneath an off-white traditional Maratha turban with a narrow red band at its base, tilted slightly forward. He wears an era-accurate off-white cotton angarkha, knee-length, front-wrapped and tied on the right side with thin cords, with fitted long sleeves and a subtle vertical weave texture. Below, show a plain off-white dhoti wrapped Maharashtrian style, pleated in front and ending mid-calf, and a deep maroon cotton sash snug around his waist with one short end hanging over his left hip. Drape a light beige cotton shawl folded over his left shoulder and around his back, and put simple brown leather kolhapuri-style sandals on his feet. Add minimal accessories: a thin dark metal bangle on his right wrist and a subtle black thread around his neck. His signature weapon is a slightly curved Indo-Persian talwar with a steel blade, brass hilt, and disc pommel, in a plain brown leather scabbard slung from his maroon sash, resting at his left hip and also shown unsheathed in one pose. Use a palette dominated by off-white, maroon, beige, brown, and brass, and present multiple angles and poses to clearly show face, turban, outfit details, and how the sword is worn.
